behavior_summary: Provides gameplay handlers for a bench item: a 'U8P_WBENCH::look' (examine) handler that makes the bench emit a short descriptive message, and a 'gotHit' handler that can change the bench's visual frame in response to a hit.

U8P_WBENCH::WBENCH::look prepares the bench process context (sets info/process-exclude), calls Item::bark to display the literal string "bench ", examines the bark return value, then suspends the process (typical item-bark lifecycle).

U8P_WBENCH::WBENCH::gotHit examines a hit-related parameter and, when that parameter is in the expected state (inferred: zero/false), calls Item::setFrame(this, 1) to update the bench's animation/frame; otherwise it returns without changing the frame.
notable_control_flow: U8P_WBENCH::WBENCH::look: initialize process context -> set process info/exclude -> push object, message, pid -> call Item::bark -> free message -> inspect retval via an 'implies' style check -> pop temp -> suspend the process. (Confidence: high)

U8P_WBENCH::WBENCH::gotHit: initialize process context -> set process info/exclude -> test third parameter ([BP+0Ah]) -> if condition met (appears to be zero/false) call Item::setFrame(this, 1) -> return. (Confidence: medium)
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the bark return value inspected by WBENCH::look (what return codes imply and whether they affect game state beyond the suspend).
Precise meaning and name of the third parameter to WBENCH::gotHit ([BP+0Ah]) — inferred as a flag or direction; unclear whether it encodes damage type, source, or a boolean.
Event numbering / trigger context for gotHit: which engine event index maps to this handler in runtime (inferred only).
Whether setting frame = 1 in gotHit triggers further game logic (e.g., destructible bench, visual damage) beyond the single setFrame call.

main_function.name: U8P_WBENCH::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the item's 'U8P_WBENCH::look' (examine) event: prepare process state, cause the bench to 'bark' the text "bench ", inspect the bark return value, and suspend the process.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — 'U8P_WBENCH::look': fired when the avatar or relevant actor examines/looks at the bench

function_map.name: U8P_WBENCH::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_WBENCH::look event handler that emits a descriptive string and manages process lifecycle for the bark action.

function_map.name: U8P_WBENCH::gotHit
function_map.role: Hit event handler that conditionally sets the bench's animation/frame to 1 based on a hit-related parameter.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item API used to display/broadcast a short item message (here used to show 'bench ').

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Engine/item API to change the visual frame/index of an item (used to set frame 1 on hit).

external_calls.purpose: Make the bench emit/display the literal string "bench " to the target process/player and participate in the item's bark lifecycle.
Set the bench object's visual frame to 1 as a response to a qualifying hit condition.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides gameplay handlers for a bench item: a 'U8P_WBENCH::look' (examine) handler that makes the bench emit a short descriptive message, and a 'gotHit' handler that can change the bench's visual frame in response to a hit.
